subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV/ECF, World Bank, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Ethiopian official (cautiously) + independent/international press (Addis Standard, Ethiopia Insight, Reuters) + HRW/Amnesty/ACLED.
A genuine but fragile reform story (a 2024 currency float, an IMF program, GERD generating, disinflation) dominated by multiple active conflicts and an acute Eritrea war-risk.

Standout drags: active insurgencies, ethnic-federal fragmentation, a reversed press opening, and an external war-risk; the demographic dividend is the main offset. Source-lean: independent/multilateral-heavy, skeptical of state framing.

economic.notes: A fragile post-default reform (a float, an IMF program) against a low tax base, high inflation, and FX constraints.

fiscal_state.notes: Defaulted in 2023-24 and in IMF/World-Bank-backed restructuring with a low tax base, but reforming. (IMF CR 26/20; Addis Standard)

monetary_independence.notes: A July-2024 float eliminated the parallel premium, with inflation-targeting attempted from an FX-short, controlled base. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The birr is not held as reserves. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Down from 30%+ to ~11-12% but creeping back, with pre-election fiscal-loosening risk. (IMF; Reuters)

banking_stability.notes: State-dominated and opening to foreign entry, with thin/fragile reserves. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Coffee/agriculture and a state-led model with doubling goods exports and GERD hydropower upside, but FX-constrained and export-weak. (IMF; Horn Review)

institutional.notes: A conflict-disrupted, contested rule of law and a reversed press opening.

rule_of_law.notes: A low and declining WJP standing, conflict-disrupted and contested under ethnic federalism. (WJP)

statistical_integrity.notes: Opaque, with IMF-flagged data gaps and unmeasured conflict zones. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A developmental-state ambition but weak and contested reach. (World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: RSF ~145/180 ('very serious'), the post-2018 opening reversed with detentions and restrictive 2025 media-law amendments. (RSF; HRW)

political_social.notes: Multiple active insurgencies and ethnic-federal fragmentation, with a demographic dividend the offset.

political_stability.notes: The 2020-22 Tigray war ended via a 2022 deal, but new Amhara ('Fano') and Oromia insurgencies persist amid emergency measures and a delayed election. (HRW; Chatham House)

civil_liberties.notes: The post-2018 thaw reversed, with detentions, conflict atrocities, and civic-space closure. (HRW; OMCT)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A major refugee host, but not a relocation destination, with strained capacity. (UNHCR context)

social_cohesion.notes: Deep ethnic-federal cleavages and multiple active insurgencies. (HRW)

demographics.notes: Very large, young, and fast-growing - a dividend-or-strain double-edge. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A pragmatic non-aligned posture, against internal insurgencies and an acute Eritrea war-risk.

alliance_reliability.notes: Non-aligned and a BRICS member, hedging the US, China, and the Gulf - pragmatic but unstable. (press)

conflict_involvement.notes: Multiple internal insurgencies, an acute Eritrea war-risk, and a Red-Sea/sea-access push straining ties with Somalia and Egypt. (Atlantic Council; Addis Standard)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: FX controls easing under reform; not broadly sanctioned. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: A state infrastructure build-out undercut by conflict, weak healthcare, and drought/flood exposure.

climate.notes: ND-GAIN bottom-25 - recurrent Horn drought plus deadly floods, a vast rain-fed population, low readiness. (ND-GAIN; WB)

infrastructure.notes: A state build-out (GERD now generating, rail, industrial parks) but with roughly half the population lacking electricity and conflict damage. (World Bank)

summary: A very large, young economy mid-way through a genuine but fragile reform - a 2024 currency float, an IMF program and debt restructuring, the GERD dam now generating, and disinflation from a high base. That story is dominated by conflict: after the Tigray war ended in 2022, new Amhara and Oromia insurgencies persist amid emergency measures and a delayed election, and an acute Eritrea war-risk plus a Red-Sea sea-access push strain regional ties. Deep ethnic-federal cleavages, a reversed press opening, weak healthcare, and half the population lacking electricity weigh heavily, with the demographic dividend the main offset.
